body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: DickSimnel provider routing parity
body.intention: I intend that DickSimnel routes inference through the same tiered cascade and provider registry as CC, not a bespoke or stale path.
body.description:
D-dsimnel-cc-parity-2026-06-04 shipped T-dsimnel-cc-parity, T-inference-caching-all-providers, T-flat-rate-provider-routing (all closed), but no consequence check was filed.

**What changed:** DickSimnel designated as CC mirror — inference routing, provider selection, and caching aligned between DickSimnel and CC.

**Predicted unintended effects:** DickSimnel may use a hardcoded model or provider path that was never updated to use the shared registry. Flat-rate routing may have shipped in the registry but DickSimnel's dispatch may still bypass it.

**Signals to watch:** Check DickSimnel's inference dispatch path — does it call the shared provider registry? Is caching active for DickSimnel sessions? Is flat-rate provider routing reflected in observed model selection?

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-23

**Affected files:** None — code audit and observation
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Verify routing parity between CC and DickSimnel at the dispatch layer. Out of scope: performance tuning.
**Test plan:** no tests — code audit ticket
**Completion criteria:** Read DickSimnel's dispatch code and confirm it routes through shared provider registry with caching enabled. Record: parity confirmed / gap found (with reason).
